metadata.desc: Image and video generation plugin supporting Gemini, OpenAI, CogVideoX, Agnes, reverse-engineered Vertex AI, and advanced configuration parameters.
metadata.short_desc: Generate and edit images and videos with multiple AI providers.

prompt.hint: Format 1: Trigger word Prompt word --Parameter 1 Parameter value 1 --Parameter 2 Parameter value 2. Format 2: [Trigger word 1, Trigger word 2] Prompt word --Parameter 1 Parameter value 1 --Parameter 2 Parameter value 2. Trigger words and trigger word lists cannot have spaces. If the parameter value is omitted, it is regarded as a Boolean value true. Placeholder prompt words {{user_text}}: The placeholder prompt words will be overwritten by the message content. Fixed preset prompt words can be added before and after the placeholder prompt words. Please refer to README.md for optional parameters. Conflicting trigger words may overwrite each other.

enabled.hint: When disabled, this provider is silently skipped, including when selected as a default provider or explicitly specified via parameters.

enabled_as_default.hint: Join the default provider set. When the user does not specify the --providers parameter, it will fall back to use according to priority from the set.

fallback_order.hint: The smaller the number, the higher the priority and will be overridden by the provider order specified directly by the --providers parameter.

base_url.hint: You can configure the reverse proxy here

recaptcha_base_api.hint: You can configure the reverse proxy here

system_prompt.hint: Leave blank to not pass this parameter.

max_refresh.hint: After obtaining the Token for the first time, the maximum number of times the Token is reacquired is allowed; when the upper limit is reached, switch to the next provider.

retry_before_switch.hint: After the cumulative number of verification failures or common request failures reaches this number, switch to the next provider.

retry_delay.hint: A larger retry delay may be able to avoid the problem of concentrated failures in a short period of time, unit: seconds

response_modalities.hint: Image-generation models may fail if ‘IMAGE’ is omitted. Some models fail if response modalities are sent at all. Select ‘None’ to omit this parameter.

enable_proxy.hint: To enable the proxy, you must first configure the proxy address in 'General Configuration'

max_images.hint: The maximum number of input images supported by this provider, after which images will be truncated. This is equivalent to the model capability, which has the highest priority. Enter -1 for no limit.

enabled_as_default.hint: Join the default provider set. When the user does not specify the --providers parameter, it will fall back to use according to priority from the set.

fallback_order.hint: The smaller the number, the higher the priority and will be overridden by the provider order specified directly by the --providers parameter.

keys.hint: API Key list. Each request is retried out of sequence.

base_url.hint: No need to fill in the /v1beta path

base_url.hint: MiniMax image-generation endpoint. You may enter the URL up to /v1 and the plugin will append image_generation automatically, or enter the full /v1/image_generation URL.

model.hint: Default image-01. minimax only supports 1 reference image

base_url.hint: By convention, this base url usually needs to be supplemented with /v1.

model.hint: For the Responses API, specify a mainline model that supports the image_generation tool, such as gpt-5.5.

enable_proxy.hint: To enable the proxy, you must first configure the proxy address in 'General Configuration'

max_images.hint: The maximum number of input images supported by this provider, after which images will be truncated. This is equivalent to the model capability, which has the highest priority. Enter -1 for no limit.

base_url.hint: Enter the URL up to /v1 and the plugin will append images/generations automatically, or enter the full /v1/images/generations URL.

model.hint: Default Kwai-Kolors/Kolors. A maximum of 3 reference images can be transferred.

image_size.hint: For example 1024x1024, 960x1280, 1664x928. Not passed when left blank or when the prompt word specifies --image_size default.

batch_size.hint: The request field is batch_size; it can be overridden with --n in the prompt word.

negative_prompt.hint: Leave blank to not pass. The prompt word can be overwritten with --negative_prompt. English commas can be used to replace spaces in the prompt words, and the backend will convert them into spaces.

num_inference_steps.hint: Leave blank to not pass. The prompt word can be overwritten with --num_inference_steps.

guidance_scale.hint: Leave blank to not pass. The prompt word can be overwritten with --guidance_scale.

seed.hint: Leave blank to not pass. The prompt word can be overwritten with --seed.

enable_proxy.hint: To enable the proxy, you must first configure the proxy address in 'General Configuration'

max_images.hint: The maximum number of input images supported by this provider, after which images will be truncated. This is equivalent to the model capability, which has the highest priority. Enter -1 for no limit.

enabled.hint: When disabled, this provider is silently skipped, including when selected as a default provider or explicitly specified via parameters.

enabled_as_default.hint: Join the default provider set. When the user does not specify the --providers parameter, it will fall back to use according to priority from the set.

fallback_order.hint: The smaller the number, the higher the priority and will be overridden by the provider order specified directly by the --providers parameter.

keys.hint: API Key list. There is no need to fill in the Bearer prefix, the backend will automatically splice it. Each request is retried out of sequence.

base_url.hint: According to the openai compatible endpoint convention, remember to add /v1 here.

enable_proxy.hint: To enable the proxy, you must first configure the proxy address in 'General Configuration'

max_images.hint: The maximum number of input images supported by this provider, after which images will be truncated. This is equivalent to the model capability, which has the highest priority. Enter -1 for no limit.

keys.hint: Created in the bigmodel.cn console, no need to fill in the Bearer prefix.

base_url.hint: Can be filled in / paas/v4, the plugin will automatically splice videos/generations and async-result.

model.hint: Mainly adapted to cogvideox-flash, and also compatible with cogvideox-2 with the same interface.

quality.hint: Speed ​​gives priority to speed and quality gives priority to quality.

size.hint: default means not passed, it is decided by the model based on the input.

watermark_enabled.hint: Disabling the watermark requires identity verification and signing the disclaimer under ‘Personal Center → Security Management → Watermark Removal Management’.

poll_interval.hint: Unit: second, minimum processing is 1 second.

job_timeout.hint: Unit: second, stop polling after timeout.

max_images.hint: CogVideoX-Flash only uses the first reference image.

default_astr_providers.hint: Even if you do not select a provider here, you can directly specify the provider of the native configuration through the --providers parameter. This configuration is only a supplement to the default provider. The priority of the native provider is fixed at 0; when sorting, the priority is ascending. Template providers with the same priority precede native providers. Template providers are arranged in the order of configuration files, and native providers are arranged in the order of this list.

params_config.hint: If no parameters are specified in the prompt word, the default parameters here will be used. Custom prompt words can also specify parameters directly, remember to include spaces. Example: bnn prompt word --image_size 4K --google_search

min_images.hint: Must be a non-negative integer. If the collected images are less than this value, and it is aiocqhttp, non-tool call, the sender's avatar will be automatically collected. If there are not enough images, the robot avatar will continue to be added. If the final number of images is still not enough, an error message will be returned.

max_images.hint: Must be a non-negative integer. If the number of images in the message exceeds this value n, the first n images will be automatically intercepted. Please refer to the corresponding provider for the upper limit of input images supported by a specific provider.

refer_images.hint: Fill in the image file names (including suffix) separated by commas. The image files are placed in the refer_images folder of the plugin data directory and will be passed to the context as reference images first. Prompt word parameter usage: --refer_images file name 1, file name 2

gather_mode.hint: After being enabled, the plugin will not start drawing immediately, allowing users to send multiple messages continuously. The collected text and images will not be used for image generation until they reply "Start". Tool calls automatically ignore this parameter.

url.hint: After enabling, when the --url parameter is not specified in the prompt word, only the image URL will be returned by default and the image will not be sent directly. --url true/false in the prompt word overrides this default.

gemini_image_config.hint: Usually only used by Gemini and Vertex provider types, but other supported providers are also able to read

aspect_ratio.hint: Setting to default will not pass this parameter. According to the Gemini API documentation, by default the model will keep the size of the output image consistent with the size of the input image. Only Gemini specifications are valid.

image_size.hint: The resolution of the generated image must be uppercase K. Currently only valid for gemini-3 models of the Gemini specification

google_search.hint: Allows models to use Google search tools to obtain real-time information. Currently only valid for gemini-3 models of the Gemini specification

openai_image_config.hint: Usually only used by the OpenAI Images provider type, but other supported providers are also able to read it.

moderation.hint: Content security audit level for GPT image editing models such as gpt-image-*. Set to low to make content filtering less sensitive.

size.hint: The resolution of the generated image. Filling in default will be automatically judged by the plugin based on the size keywords and the first image entered by the user. The priority is: keywords > first image. If there is no image input and no keywords, the default is auto.

size_keyword_map.hint: It only takes effect when the resolution is default. Format: Keyword 1, Keyword 2: Resolution. For example, mobile phone, vertical screen: 1024x1536. Match in list order, with the rule matching first taking precedence.

partial_images.hint: OpenAI Images The number of preview images returned during streaming response, ranging from 0-3. 0 means only the final image is returned. Only takes effect when streaming responses are enabled.

params_alias_map.hint: The format is custom name: built-in parameter name. After setting, both the custom name and the built-in parameter name can be used to apply parameters. Conflicting parameter names may overwrite each other.

preset_append.description: Append User Text to Preset Prompts
preset_append.hint: When enabled, user input text will be appended after the preset prompt word. It is equivalent to adding the {{user_text}} placeholder to the end of the default prompt word. If there is already a {{user_text}} placeholder, it will be ignored.

smart_retry.hint: After enabling, retries are skipped for some response codes and the next Key is switched directly.

max_retry.hint: The maximum number of retries for each Key

timeout.hint: Unit: seconds, only valid for model requests. The timeout is calculated independently for each retry.

proxy.hint: Format example: http://127.0.0.1:7890. Filling in this item will not cause the proxy to be enabled by default. It needs to be explicitly enabled in other configuration items.

strip_metadata.hint: Before sending the reference image, try to erase the metadata containing privacy as much as possible. If the processing fails, the reference image will be removed. Multi-frame images (such as GIF) are currently not supported and will be removed directly.

image_hosting.hint: Used in --url mode to upload images that only return base64 to your own image bed and then return to the public link.

enabled.hint: Only valid for --url mode. When the provider returns the URL natively, no upload is required.

upload_url.hint: Example: https://your-worker.your-subdomain.workers.dev Do not manually add the file name at the end.

public_base_url.hint: If the same Worker domain name is used for uploading and accessing, you can fill in the same address as upload_url.

auth_token.hint: It will be sent as the request header X-Auth-Token, corresponding to your Cloudflare Worker environment variable X_AUTH_TOKEN.

path_prefix.hint: Uploaded files will be saved under this prefix, such as big-banana/2026/06/01/uuid.jpg.

prefix_config.hint: By default, @bot will ignore the prefix requirement (equivalent to enabling hybrid mode)

coexist_enabled.hint: After enabling, the image generation function can be triggered both without command prefix and with command prefix. There is no need to enable it if there are no special requirements.

prefix_list.hint: List of command prefixes. The trigger format is command prefix + trigger word.

provider_prefix.hint: When enabled, it will be checked whether the provider name is included after the command prefix (if any) and before the default prompt word. Overrides the default provider and --providers parameters if present.

preference_config.hint: The following configurations vary depending on individual usage needs, and there is currently no one-size-fits-all solution. The three options for skipping the At avatar share a count.

skip_at_first.hint: When waking up a robot via @, skip collecting the robot's avatar once.

skip_quote_first.hint: If the @ object is the sender of the referenced message, the avatar collection of the @ object is skipped once.

skip_llm_at_first.description: LLM Tool: Skip the First @Bot Avatar
skip_llm_at_first.hint: Trigger the LLM function call tool through @robot to skip collecting the robot's avatar once.

enable_at_avatar_note.hint: After enabling, when the @ object avatar is added as a reference image, an English number description will be appended to the bottom of the prompt word before final generation, helping the AI ​​to determine which image in the final input image list each @ corresponds to. After closing, the avatar reference image will still be added, but this description will not be added.

enable_drawing_message.hint: After enabling it, after sending the trigger command, a generating prompt message will be sent first, and then the image will be generated.

enable_llm_tool_drawing_message.description: Send Progress Message for LLM Tools
enable_llm_tool_drawing_message.hint: After enabling it, after sending the trigger command, a generating prompt message will be sent first, and then the image will be generated.

drawing_message.hint: Prompt text sent to the user when starting to generate images.

send_text_when_no_image.description: Send Text Responses When No Image Is Returned
send_text_when_no_image.hint: After turning on, if the response does not contain an image but contains text content, the text content will be sent as the result; otherwise, "the response does not contain image data" will be returned.

group_cooldown.hint: Controls the cooling time (in seconds) for each group to use drawing tools, 0 means no limit. The cooldown calculation starts after the image is successfully sent.

command_use_background_task.description: Run Command Image Generation as a Background Task
command_use_background_task.hint: After it is turned on, drawing will be executed as a background task and the results will be sent back after completion; after it is turned off, it will wait for the drawing to be completed in the current message processing process. Only command calls take effect.

background_task_send_type.hint: How to send messages after the background task is completed. event: event message (passive message, default); active: active message.

gather_timeout.hint: The maximum time (seconds) to wait for the user to send a image or trigger an end command after sending a message. The remaining time is reset after each collection.

enable_preset_tool.hint: Allows LLM to query available presets and preset prompt word content

enable_image_generation_tool.hint: Allows LLM to generate images based on text, chat images, or avatar references. If you enable this option, make sure at least one image provider is enabled.

enable_video_generation_tool.hint: Allows LLM to generate a video based on text and an optional reference image. If you enable this, make sure at least one video provider is enabled.

llm_tool_preset_name.hint: Fill in the trigger words in the default prompt word configuration. This preset overwrites the plugin default parameters by field; LLM calls the specified preset this time and then overwrites this preset by field. Parameters that are not specified by the subsequent level will continue to be retained. Leave it blank to not use it.

llm_video_tool_preset_name.hint: Fill in the preset trigger words for video generation. By default, the plugin built-in llm_video_default is used. When left blank, the tool parameters are used directly.

llm_tool_direct_send_result.description: Let LLM Tools Send Image Results Directly
llm_tool_direct_send_result.hint: When it is turned off, the image result is returned to AI or the upstream callback, which decides to send, regenerate or perform other operations; when it is turned on, the plugin sends the image directly, and then returns the text completion status to AI or the upstream callback.

llm_tool_truncate_images.description: Limit Returned Images to the Requested Count
llm_tool_truncate_images.hint: After turning on, if the number of images actually returned by the image provider exceeds the current generation quantity ( --n ), only the first n images will be passed to the AI, upstream callback or chat window; when the generation quantity is not set this time, the default is 1.

llm_tool_restrict_private_network.description: Restrict LLM Tool Access to Private-Network Image URLs
llm_tool_restrict_private_network.hint: When enabled, refuse to resolve remote image URLs to intranet, loopback, or reserved addresses. If the proxy software Fake-IP mode is used and the public network images are misjudged, it can be closed; after closing, please bear the security risk of LLM accessing the intranet URL.

llm_tool_use_background_task.description: Run Tool Calls as Background Tasks
llm_tool_use_background_task.hint: After being enabled, BigBanana will perform background tasks inside the plugin. When configuring the upstream callback, hand the result to the callback; when enabling direct sending, actively send the image after completion. When neither is configured, it will automatically change to the foreground execution to avoid loss of image results.

background_callback_plugin.hint: The name of the upstream plugin that receives the results after background drawing is completed. It will only take effect if filled in together with the background callback method. Fill in astrbot_plugin_giftia when using Giftia. If the callback call fails, it will automatically fall back to active sending.

background_callback_method.hint: The method name in the upstream plugin; the method name defined by astrbot_plugin_giftia is on_drawing_complete. It will be called with five keyword parameters: event, result, params, unified_msg_origin and is_success. result is always MessageChain; params are the final generation parameters; unified_msg_origin is the original session ID snapshot when the task is submitted; is_success indicates whether the generation is successful. When direct sending is not enabled, the result contains unsent media or failure text; when enabled, the plugin will send the media first, and the result only contains the text completion status. The method can be a synchronous function, coroutine, or asynchronous generator; explicitly returning False indicates that it has not been taken over and triggers BigBanana fallback processing, and returning True or a non-bool value indicates that it has been taken over.

local_save.hint: The generated images are saved to the save_images folder in the plugin data directory.

r2_save.hint: After generation, upload the image to the R2 image bed configured in "Image bed upload", which does not affect the normal sending of the image. Image bed upload needs to be enabled and fully configured first. Only upload once when used with --url mode.

sub_brain.hint: After enabling it, when the large dialogue model calls the drawing tool to generate a image, the short description passed to the tool will be submitted to the assistant brain for expansion and optimization, and then to the drawing provider to generate the image.

cmd_enabled.description: Enable Prompt Optimization for Commands by Default
cmd_enabled.hint: Whether the command call enables the side brain by default. After it is turned off, only the prompt word explicitly uses the --sub_brain parameter to enable the side brain optimization.

tool_enabled.description: Enable Prompt Optimization for Tools by Default
tool_enabled.hint: Whether the tool call enables the side brain by default. After it is turned off, only the prompt word explicitly uses the --sub_brain parameter to enable the side brain optimization.

provider_id.hint: LLM provider for optimizing prompt words. Leave blank to use the provider currently used by the current conversation.

system_prompt.hint: System prompt words that guide the accessory brain to optimize prompt words

enabled.hint: After turning it on, only groups in the allowlist can use it.

whitelist.hint: The allowlist group UMO list can be obtained by using /sid in the corresponding group. UMO must be written completely.

user_enabled.hint: After turning it on, only users in the allowlist can use it.

only_for_commands.hint: When turned on, the allowlist only intercepts direct commands to draw, and LLM triggering is not restricted by interception.

admin.title: Image Generation Settings
admin.heading: Big Banana Image Generation Settings
admin.subtitle: Configure image generation providers, parameters, allowlists, and command prefixes

presets.title: Preset Prompt Rules
presets.description: Configure preset prompts for trigger words. Both a single trigger and a [word1,word2] list are supported. The {{user_text}} placeholder is replaced with the user's input.

providers.title: Image Provider Priority
providers.description: Select and reorder providers to set their priority. If the first provider fails, the plugin automatically falls back to the next one. Leave empty to use the provider from the current conversation.

video_providers.title: Video Provider Priority
video_providers.description: Video presets only use providers with the video_generation capability.

min_images.label: Minimum Input Images
min_images.hint: If too few images are provided, the mentioned user's or sender's avatar is added automatically.

max_images.label: Maximum Input Images
max_images.hint: The plugin does not impose a global limit; the actual limit depends on the selected provider.

aspect_ratio.label: Default Aspect Ratio
aspect_ratio.hint: Set the output image aspect ratio. This only applies to providers that support this parameter.

moderation.label: Content Moderation Level
moderation.hint: Content safety filtering level for GPT image-editing models such as gpt-image-*. Set it to low to reduce filtering sensitivity.

google_search.label: Allow the Model to Use Google Search

refer_images.label: Reference Image Filenames
refer_images.hint: Separate filenames with commas and place the images in the refer_images folder under the plugin data directory.

url_only.label: Return Image URLs Only by Default
url_only.hint: The --url true/false option in a prompt overrides this default.

preset_append.label: Append User Input to Preset Prompts
preset_append.hint: If a prompt does not contain the {{user_text}} placeholder, the user's input is appended automatically.

prefix.title: Prefixes and Trigger Modes
prefix.coexist_enabled: Enable Mixed Mode (With or Without Prefix)

preferences.title: Trigger Preferences
preferences.skip_at_first: Skip the First Mentioned Bot Avatar
preferences.skip_quote_first: Skip the First Quoted Sender Avatar
preferences.skip_llm_at_first: LLM Tool Calls: Skip the First Mentioned Avatar
preferences.send_text_when_no_image: Send Text Responses When No Image Is Returned
preferences.send_text_when_no_image_hint: When enabled, a response containing text but no image is sent as the result.
preferences.group_cooldown_hint: Set the cooldown between image commands or tool calls for each group. Use 0 for no limit.
preferences.use_background_task: Run Image Generation as a Background Task
preferences.use_background_task_hint: When disabled, the plugin waits for image generation to finish before sending the result.
preferences.background_task_send_type_hint: Choose how completed background tasks send messages. Event messages (passive) are used by default.

tools.title: Function-Calling Tools
tools.use_background_task: Run Tool Calls as Background Tasks
tools.use_background_task_hint: When disabled, function calls wait for image generation to finish before returning.

scope.title: Allowlist Scope
scope.only_for_commands: Apply the Allowlist to Commands Only (LLM Triggers Are Not Blocked)

storage.title: Image Storage
storage.local: Store Images in the Local Plugin Data Directory

sub_brain.title: Prompt Optimization Assistant
sub_brain.description: When enabled, short descriptions passed by the conversation model to the image generation tool are expanded and refined by a secondary LLM before being sent to the image provider.
sub_brain.provider_hint: Select the LLM provider used to optimize prompts. Leave empty to use the provider from the current conversation.
sub_brain.system_prompt_placeholder: System prompt that guides prompt optimization

aliases.title: Parameter Alias Mapping (Params Alias Map)
aliases.description: Map custom aliases to built-in parameter names so users can invoke image generation parameters by their aliases.

persona_replace.title: Persona Avatar Replacement
persona_replace.description: Map a target ID (such as a bot or user QQ ID, or the aliases "bot" and "self") to a list of reference avatars. When triggered, the system randomly selects one image. Image URLs and local uploads are supported.

persona.target_placeholder: Enter a QQ ID or bot / self

toast.read_failed: Failed to read the image file
toast.save_success: Configuration saved. Reload the plugin for changes to take effect.

errors.config_save_failed: Failed to save the basic configuration
errors.substitutions_save_failed: Failed to save persona replacement settings

validation.non_negative_images: Minimum and maximum input image counts must be non-negative integers
validation.empty_prompt: The prompt body for preset “{trigger}” cannot be empty
